overview: The UG Elves deck is a fast, aggressive tribal strategy that wins by flooding the board with low-cost creatures to deal lethal damage in a single strike or overwhelm opponents with mana and life gain. Despite facing targeted removal and sweepers, it maintains resilience through draw spells and a low land count. It is considered a strong, beloved (or hated) archetype in the current Pauper metagame.
win_conditions: Deal lethal damage in a single attack with a large board of Elves
Generate massive mana to play multiple creatures per turn
Gain significant life to outlast attrition while applying pressure

keep_criteria: Hands with 1 land are often sufficient if they contain draw spells
Hands that can apply early pressure or fit Lead the Stampede or Distant Melody
Keep hands with 1-2 lands and at least one mana dork (Llanowar/Fyndhorn).
Prioritize hands that can play a dork on turn 1.
mulligan_criteria: Hands lacking lands or draw spells that cannot establish early board presence
Mulligan to 4+ lands with no dorks, or hands with only dorks and no lands.

examples.reason: A hand with 1 land is enough to put up a good work and apply pressure on the opponent, especially when fitting a draw spell.

early_game: Adopt an aggressive 'all win' posture to close out games quickly
Play early mana dorks like Wellwisher to generate life and pressure
Turn 1: Play land, then Llanowar Elves.
Turn 2: Play land, then Fyndhorn Elves or second Llanowar.
Turn 3: Play a big threat (e.g., Avenging Hunter) or pump spell, using 3-4 mana.
Sequence lands to ensure you can cast your threat on time.
mid_game: Play enough Elves to win without necessarily committing your entire board at once
Use draw spells to refill your hand and maintain pressure after playing multiple creatures
Prioritize mana acceleration over card draw early.
Use pump spells efficiently to maximize combat damage.
Don't overcommit to board if opponent has sweepers.
Commit threats when you have enough mana to answer or out-value removal.
Hold back if opponent has potential sweepers or hate.
Use combat tricks to trade favorably.
late_game: If the game stalls, use sideboarded artifacts like Viridian Longbow to lock down the board
Continue flooding the board to overwhelm opponents with mana and creature count
Avenging Hunter + pump spells, or multiple large creatures with +1/+1 counters.
Win through combat or direct damage if needed.
Go all-in when you have 5+ mana, opponent has no sweepers, and you can close the game in 1-2 turns.

key_cards.name: Jaspera Sentinel
key_cards.notes: 1 cost with a 1/2 body, having reach to block creatures with flying - especially faeries. Also, it's another colored mana source.

key_cards.name: Sylvan Ranger
key_cards.notes: Guarantees the Island is played, enabling counterspells to protect the board.

key_cards.name: Lys Alana Huntmaster
key_cards.notes: Costs 4 mana but helps generate enough mana to play multiple creatures per turn.

key_cards.name: Distant Melody
key_cards.notes: Can draw 10 or more cards, refilling the hand without taking up a sideboard slot.

key_cards.name: Lead the Stampede
key_cards.notes: Allows the player to easily return to the game after board wipes or removal.

key_cards.name: Gleeful Sabotage
key_cards.notes: Used against Makeshift Munitions or specific enchantments that provide trample, protection, or lifegain.

key_cards.name: Scattershot Archer
key_cards.notes: Effective against Faeries decks to clear out flying threats.

key_cards.name: Spidersilk Armor
key_cards.notes: Provides protection against targeted removal and sweepers from Faeries decks.

key_cards.name: Viridian Longbow
key_cards.notes: Makes a difference in locked games, but less effective if the opponent can still generate mana and play creatures.

key_cards.name: Wellwisher
key_cards.role: Early game life gain / Mana dork
key_cards.notes: Needed early to gain more life than the opponent can deal, especially against Bogles.

sequencing_tips: Avoid playing your entire board at once to prevent losing everything to a single sweeper
Prioritize playing enough Elves to secure a win while conserving resources
Play carefully around threats like Krark-Clan Shaman that can easily clean the board
Use draw spells to refill your hand after committing multiple creatures to the battlefield
Always count opponent's mana and board state.
Use pump spells to maximize value.
Avenging Hunter likely has a specific ability

matchups.advice: Favorable matchup as Elves are natural predators; destroying lands doesn't work well against mana-generating creatures. Sideboard out Lead the Stampede post-side to avoid dumping it.
Play carefully around board cleaners. Use Gleeful Sabotage specifically against Makeshift Munitions rather than lands.
Get Wellwisher early to gain life faster than they can deal damage. Use Gleeful Sabotage to remove key enchantments.
Use Jaspera Sentinel early to block flying threats. Sideboard in Scattershot Archer, Spidersilk Armor, and Viridian Longbow.
Game 1 is slightly better as sweepers are usually sideboarded. Use Jaspera Sentinel and sideboard similarly to Dimir.
Whoever plays Lead the Stampede, Distant Melody, or Timberwatch Elf first usually wins. Sideboard in Viridian Longbow only if the game is locked.
Use cheap, efficient threats. Cast dorks early to build mana advantage. Use pump spells in response to counters if needed.
Out-value them with card draw or efficient removal. Don't overextend; wait for them to tap out.
Play early threats to race. Use pump spells to stabilize. Bring in small creatures or removal post-sideboard.
Apply early pressure. Don't let them stabilize. Use card advantage to outlast them.
Win the mana race. Prioritize dorks and efficient threats. Use disruption to slow their ramp. Trade favorably in combat.

common_threats: Watch for sweepers (Wrath of God, Damnation), hate cards (Cage, Bog), and counters.
Note when opponent taps out for a big spell vs. holding mana for interaction.

overview_evidence.evidence_span: Our posture must be that of an Aggro player... The strength of the Elves, united, is overwhelming, and it can be very fast.

win_conditions_evidence.evidence_span: you've definitely lost to Elves in just one attack and taking a lot of damage
play more than 10 creatures, gain a lot of life or win the game with just one strike

sequencing_tips_evidence.evidence_span: With Elves, we need to remember and always think: 'I don't want to lose my entire board at once'. With that in mind, let's play enough Elves to win the game.
Krark-Clan Shaman — which easily cleans our board, and we can't interact so easily - so play carefully around it.

common_threats_evidence.evidence_span: many targeted removals and sweepers like Fiery Cannonade and Suffocating Fumes
Krark-Clan Shaman — which easily cleans our board
Makeshift Munitions , which gives us a huge headache
Spellstutter Sprite , which counters almost everything in our deck
Dimir usually has Suffocating Fumes and even Echoing Decay in the maindeck
Izzet version will have Fiery Cannonade and even Electrickery
